response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with ~15 years of experience and a solid track record in distributed systems, low-latency services, TDD, and DevOps. However, this role centers on hands-on modern C++/STL development for industrial automation, and the candidate's professional C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments over a decade ago. There is no evidence of IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle experience, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA industrial protocols, or industrial automation domain work, all of which are central to the position. The primary languages (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) do not align with the core C++ requirement.
strengths: Extensive experience with high-performance, low-latency distributed and event-driven systems (millions of requests/sec)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, concurrency (async I/O, fibers), and multi-threading concepts
Broad full-stack and DevOps background with proven ownership (founder, open-source platform maintainer)
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience, dated to 2015; no modern C++/STL depth shown
No exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA, or industrial automation domain
Core skill set (TypeScript/Node/DLang) misaligned with the C++-centric requirements
